title: Pharmaceutical combinations comprising a histone deacetylase inhibitor and an aurora kinase inhibitor and methods of use thereof
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a pharmaceutical combination comprising (a) a histone deacetylase 6 inhibitor and (b) an aurora kinase inhibitor, including combined preparations and pharmaceutical compositions thereof; uses of such combination in the treatment or prevention of cancer; and methods of treating or preventing cancer in a subject comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of such combination.
claims: Claims 1. A pharmaceutical combination comprising: (a) a histone deacetylase 6 (HDAC6) inhibitor of Formula I: (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, wherein, ring B is aryl or heteroaryl; Ri is aryl or heteroaryl, each of which may be optionally substituted by OH, halo, or C h alky!; and R is H or Ci-e-alkyl; and (b) an aurora kinase inhibitor selected from the group consisting of alisertib, AT-9283, barasertib, ENMD-2076, MK-5108, MSC 1992371A, danusertib, and tozasertib, or pharmaceutically acceptable salts thereof. 2. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 1 , wherein the compound of Formula I is: or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 3. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 1 , wherein the compound of Formula I is: or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 4. The pharmaceutical combination of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the aurora kinase inhibitor is alisertib, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 5. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 1 , wherein the HDAC6 inhibitor and the aurora kinase inhibitor are in the same formulation. 6. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 1 , wherein the HDAC6 inhibitor and the aurora kinase inhibitor are in separate formulations. 7. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 1 , wherein the combination is for simultaneous or sequential administration. 8. The pharmaceutical combination of any one of claims 1-7 for use in treating cancer in a subject in need thereof. 9. The pharmaceutical combination of any one of claims 1-7 for use in the preparation of a medicament for the treatment of cancer. 10. The pharmaceutical combination of claim 8 or 9, wherein the cancer is a lymphoma, leukemia, or myeloma. 11. The pharmaceutical combination of any one of claims 8 or 9, wherein the cancer is a solid tumor. 12. A method for treating or preventing cancer in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of (a) a histone deacetylase 6 (HDAC6) inhibitor of Formula I: (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, wherein, ring B is aryl or heteroaryl; Ri is aryl or heteroaryl, each of which may be optionally substituted by OH, halo, or C h alky!; and R is H or Ci -6 -alkyl; and (b) an aurora kinase inhibitor selected from the group consisting of alisertib, AT-9283, barasertib, ENMD-2076, MK-5108, MSC 1992371A, danusertib, and tozasertib, or pharmaceutically acceptable salts thereof.. The method of claim 12, wherein the compound of Formula I is: or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the compound of Formula I is: or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 15. The method of any one of claims 12-14, wherein the aurora kinase inhibitor is alisertib, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. The method of claim 12, wherein the cancer is a lymphoma, leukemia, or myeloma. 17. The method of claim 12, wherein the cancer is a solid tumor. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the HDAC6 inhibitor and the aurora kinase inhibitor are administered at approximately the same time. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the HDAC6 inhibitor and the aurora kinase inhibitor are administered at different times. 20. A method of decreasing cell growth and viability, comprising contacting the cell with a pharmaceutical combination of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the cell is a myeloma cell or a lymphoma cell.
